title: Magnificently Sour Kenyan Cherry Skewers
description: A satvik-friendly Kenyan dish featuring sour cherries and a deglaze-fond, perfect for a unique dessert or snack. This aerated treat is sure to impress with its show-off presentation.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together lemon juice, honey, ginger, and cardamom.
Add the cherries to the bowl and toss to coat with the lemon mixture.
Let the cherries sit for 10 minutes to allow them to release their juice.
Thread the cherries onto the bamboo skewers, leaving a small space between each cherry.
Place the skewers on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Sprinkle the baking soda over the cherries.
Bake the skewers in the preheated oven for 15 minutes.
While the skewers are baking, whip the coconut cream until it becomes light and airy.
Remove the skewers from the oven and let them cool for 5 minutes.
Drizzle the whipped coconut cream over the skewers and serve immediately.
